paper_title: Comparison of the LSM and SEM methods

methods.method_name: Pollen Evaluation Using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM)
methods.description: This method involves using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) to evaluate pollen grains, focusing on the fine relief (sculpture) of pollen and spore surfaces. SEM provides high-resolution images and is considered a significant advancement in palynology.

steps.description: Prepare pollen samples for SEM imaging, including sputtering if necessary.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples for SEM imaging

steps.description: Capture SEM images of pollen at various magnifications, including up to 8545x for detailed evaluation.
steps.searchable_summary: capture SEM images of pollen at various magnifications

steps.description: Analyze SEM images to evaluate surface morphology and resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze SEM images for surface morphology

methods.method_name: Pollen Evaluation Using Laser Scanning Microscopy (LSM)
methods.description: This method uses Laser Scanning Microscopy (LSM) to evaluate pollen grains, allowing for high-resolution imaging and morphological determination of pollen classes. LSM is efficient, easy to use, and minimizes artifacts.

steps.description: Collect fresh pollen samples for LSM evaluation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fresh pollen samples for LSM

steps.description: Evaluate pollen samples using LSM at magnifications of 1179x, 2350x, and occasionally up to 8545x.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate pollen samples using LSM at various magnifications

steps.description: Analyze LSM images to determine pollen class and identify species-specific differences.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze LSM images for pollen class determination

steps.description: Apply color-setting to LSM images to improve visibility of details.
